title: route qwen-vision to instruct variant (non-thinking)
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
PR #10969 added qwen-vision-pro (235b thinking) but left both qwen-vision sizes pointing to thinking variants. The first commit on that PR was originally going to swap the 30b to the instruct variant — that part got dropped.

This restores the intent: one fast non-thinking vision model + one deep thinking one.

- `qwen-vision`: `qwen/qwen3-vl-30b-a3b-thinking` → `qwen/qwen3-vl-30b-a3b-instruct`
- `qwen-vision-pro`: unchanged (235b thinking)
- Registry updated: `reasoning: false`, completion cost $1.56→$0.52/M, contextLength 262144→131072, aliases + description

Battle-tested upstream `qwen3-vl-30b-a3b-instruct`:
- 0 reasoning tokens on math prompt (was 374 on thinking variant)
- vision input + multi-image + tool calls all work
- max_tokens=1 → finish=length, streaming → 18 SSE chunks
- burst n=5/15/30 → 0 errors, p95 1.9-3s

Tests: aliases (186 passed), generation-vcr (12 passed).

gist.headline: Less thinking, more seeing
gist.blurb: We've swapped the standard qwen-vision model to its lightning-fast instruct variant. Now you have a snappy option for quick visual tasks, while qwen-vision-pro handles the deep thinking.
gist.summary: Routed the standard qwen-vision endpoint to qwen/qwen3-vl-30b-a3b-instruct instead of the thinking variant, dropping completion costs from $1.56 to $0.52/M tokens and adjusting context length to 131k.
gist.impact: Users calling qwen-vision will get much faster, non-reasoning responses for standard visual tasks, while qwen-vision-pro remains available for complex, reasoning-heavy image analysis.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee organizing two different camera-bots. One is sleek and fast (instruct), the other is large and glowing with deep thought (pro).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny director's cap, organizing two chunky retro robots with camera lenses for eyes. One robot looks sleek and fast, while the other sits in a deep thinking pose with a glowing screen. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm.
